title: Pseudo-query service for V6 database with file and method metadata
purpose: Provides query interfaces to access file and method metadata from a V6 SQLite database, supporting search, references, call graphs, coverage analysis, and statistics.
module_context: Defines constants for coverage extensions and excludes, and utility functions for file stem derivation and filesystem walking.

steps.content: Check if the file name includes '.test.' or '.spec.'.
IF the file name ends with '.d.ts', return true.
ELSE return false.
Extract the base name from the file path.
Determine the file extension.
IF an extension exists, remove it from the base name.
ELSE return the base name as-is.
Query the database for file metadata and method counts.
Group results by file path and aggregate method and export counts.
Map database rows to pseudo-file summary objects.
Retrieve the file row from the database using the file path.
IF the file does not exist, return null.
Query the database for methods associated with the file.
For each method, retrieve its steps and calls from the database.
Map database rows to pseudo-method objects with metadata.
Build and return a pseudo-file with methods.
Query the database for all file paths.
For each file path, derive its stem and compare with the requested stem.
IF a matching file is found, retrieve its full metadata.
ELSE return null.

methods.name: search
methods.normalized_params: (db: Database, query: string, limit?: number): SearchResult[]

steps.content: Trim the search query and return empty results if it is empty.
Escape special characters in the query for full-text search.
Execute a full-text search against the pseudo-fts table.
IF the search fails, return empty results.
Map search results to pseudo-search result objects.

methods.name: getReferences
methods.normalized_params: (db: Database, name: string, fileStem?: string): Array<{ filePath: string; methodName: string; line: number }>

steps.content: Build a SQL query to find methods that call the specified function.
IF a file stem is provided, filter results by file path pattern.
Execute the query and map results to reference objects.
Query the database for all methods to build graph nodes.
Map method rows to graph node objects.
Query the database for method call relationships to build graph edges.
Map call rows to graph edge objects.
Return the constructed graph with nodes and edges.
Query the database for all exported methods.
For each exported method, retrieve its steps and calls from the database.
Map database rows to pseudo-method objects with metadata.

methods.name: getImpactAnalysis
methods.normalized_params: (db: Database, name: string, fileStem?: string): { direct: AffectedItem[]; transitive: AffectedItem[] }

steps.content: Build a recursive SQL query to find methods that call the specified function.
IF a file stem is provided, filter results by file path pattern.
Execute the query and process results to separate direct and transitive impacts.
Map results to affected item objects with depth information.
Query the database for methods that are not exported and have no callers.
For each orphaned method, retrieve its steps and calls from the database.
Map database rows to pseudo-method objects with metadata.
Check if the project directory exists.
IF the directory does not exist, return empty coverage report.
Recursively walk the project directory to collect all source files.
Query the database for all indexed file paths.
Compare source files with indexed files to count coverage.
Calculate coverage percentage and return the report.
Read directory entries and handle read errors.
Skip entries in the exclude list or named '__tests__'.
Skip hidden entries except the current directory.
For each entry, determine if it is a directory or file.
Recursively walk directories.
For files, check if they match coverage extensions and are not test files.
Add matching files to the output array.

methods.name: getSourceLink
methods.normalized_params: (db: Database, name: string, fileStem?: string): SourceLinkCandidate[]

steps.content: Build a SQL query to find methods with the specified name and line numbers.
IF a file stem is provided, filter results by file path pattern.
Execute the query and map results to source link candidate objects.
Query the database for methods in the specified file.
Order methods by line number.
Map database rows to function objects with source metadata.
Query the database for counts of files, methods, and exported methods.
Map database row to statistics report object.
Query the database for files under the specified directory.
Group results by file path and aggregate method and export counts.
Map database rows to pseudo-file summary objects.

methods.name: getMethodLocation
methods.normalized_params: (db: Database, methodId: string): { filePath: string; line: number } | null

steps.content: Query the database for the file path and line number of the method.
IF the method does not exist, return null.
ELSE return the file path and line number.
